task_summary: Run new AI-Audit (R1+R2 multi-LLM debate procedure per CLAUDE.md) on proposal to restore Ozon sibling-fallback after SSOT cleanup regressed coverage 364→149 SKU.
deviations_from_plan: Per CLAUDE.md procedure R2 may be skipped if R1 is unanimous. R1 was NOT unanimous (1 APPROVE + 2 REJECT), so ran R2 as required. No deviation.
self_quality_rationale: Procedure followed correctly (3-role R1 parallel, isolated; R2 debate with anti-conformity rule; arbiter verdict REVISE). ADVOCATE genuinely flipped on data-driven evidence — strong signal procedure works. Artifact saved to correct path. Minus 2 points: did not double-check CHALLENGER model assignment (CLAUDE.md says Gemini 2.5 Flash but I used Haiku 4.5 for both CHALLENGER and SECOND OPINION — because no Gemini MCP/subagent was available in session). Did not record R1/R2 full verdicts inside the artifact verbatim, only summaries.
open_issues: Owner must choose: Variant C (gated sibling-fallback with ≥3 siblings + audit flag) or Variant A (accept regression, improve Haiku matcher).
CHALLENGER should ideally be Gemini 2.5 Flash per CLAUDE.md spec; used Haiku as fallback — may reduce diversity.
Sibling-heterogeneity stats (23% of leaves map to 2+ Ozon categories) should be persisted in a repeatable script, not just quoted in audit artifact.
notable_decisions: Proceeded with audit without asking 'is this worth it?' despite being a reversible change — owner explicitly said 'прогони через него еще раз' which is the hard trigger per CLAUDE.md §AI-AUDIT.
Arbiter verdict is REVISE (synthesized Variant C) rather than picking between A or B — because all three R2 auditors converged on gated middle ground.
Used Haiku 4.5 for both CHALLENGER and SECOND OPINION instead of Gemini Flash for CHALLENGER — pragmatic because no Gemini MCP was wired in this session.
